name: uart_tx
description: UART transmitter that accepts a byte or stream word and emits start, data, and stop bits on a serial tx line.

source_refs.notes: AXI-stream-style UART transmitter with prescale-driven bit timing.
Small pedagogical FSM used by the local demo generator.

parameters.name: DATA_WIDTH
parameters.description: Number of payload bits per UART frame.

parameters.name: CLKS_PER_BIT
parameters.description: Clock cycles per serial bit in simple fixed-baud implementations.

ports.name: prescale
ports.description: Baud-rate prescale or bit timing control.

states.name: IDLE
states.description: Hold tx high, advertise ready, wait for valid payload.

states.name: START
states.description: Drive start bit low for one bit time.

states.name: DATA
states.description: Shift payload bits LSB first for DATA_WIDTH bit times.

states.name: STOP
states.description: Drive stop bit high and return to idle.

constraints: txd must idle high when reset or idle.
Only accept new input when the transmitter is ready or idle.
Data bits are transmitted LSB first unless the spec explicitly requests another order.
Do not change txd faster than the configured bit period.
patterns: Latch input data only when valid and ready are both true.
Use a prescale counter to hold each serial bit for an exact number of clocks.
Keep tx high during reset and idle to match UART line convention.
Expose busy separately from ready so software/testbench can observe frame progress.
implementation_notes: Use a baud/prescale counter plus a bit counter; do not derive a separate generated clock for txd.
Latch the input word at the start handshake so later input changes do not corrupt the frame.
Represent the frame as start bit, DATA_WIDTH data bits, and stop bit, or use explicit START/DATA/STOP FSM states.
Keep busy asserted from accepted input through the stop bit.
test_strategy: Send a byte with alternating bits such as 8'hA5.
Sample tx near the center of each bit period.
Check start bit low, payload LSB first, stop bit high, and busy deassertion.
verification_goals: txd is high during reset and idle.
A transmitted frame contains one low start bit, DATA_WIDTH LSB-first data bits, and one high stop bit.
s_axis_tready or equivalent ready is low while the frame is in progress.
busy deasserts after the stop bit completes.
